differences.description: The document title changed from "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual: Volume II" to "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual, Volume II", and the release line changed from "Version 20250508: This document is in Ratified state." to "Version 20260120: Official Release".
The contributor list ending punctuation was corrected from "Reinoud Zandijk.." to "Reinoud Zandijk."
The footer document title changed from "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual: Volume II" to "The RISC-V Instruction Set Manual, Volume II".

A new current preface for Version 20260120 replaced the Version 20250508 current preface, stating that all listed ISA modules have been ratified instead of describing Ratified/Frozen/Draft status categories.
The current module list adds "Svrsw60t59b Extension" version 1.0.

The current module list names "Smctr Extension", "Svvptc Extension", and "Pointer-Masking Extensions"; the first document used "Smctr", "Svvptc", and "Pointer Masking" in the current 20250508 module list.
A change note was added: "Addition of the Svrsw60t59b extension for additional PTE reserved-for-software bits."
The 20250508 preface was moved from the current preface to a historical preface, and the module names were normalized to include "Extension" for Smctr and Svvptc and "Pointer-Masking Extensions".

In the list of changes since 20241101, the entries for Ssqosid and Pointer Masking were punctuated and renamed to "Addition of the Ssqosid Extension for Quality-of-Service Identifiers." and "Addition of the Pointer-Masking Extensions."
The compatible-change bullet about synchronization requirements was expanded from "changing the PBMTE fields in menvcfg and henvcfg" to "changing the PBMTE and ADUE fields in menvcfg and henvcfg".
A compatible-change bullet was added: "Defined the format of the memory-mapped msip registers."
The bullet "Defined optional mseccfg and mseccfgh CSRs" was changed to "Defined mseccfg and mseccfgh CSRs", removing the statement that they are optional.
The cross-reference for Type-2 hypervisor execution was changed from "as described in Chapter 21" to the section title "as described in \"H\" Extension for Hypervisor Support".
The debug specification was changed from "The separate debug specification proposal" to "The separate debug specification", removing the word "proposal".
The explanation of csr[9:8] was expanded to state that the pattern 10 represents hypervisor CSRs.

The CSR listing introduction changed from "Table 4-Table 8" to "Table 4-Table 7" for direct CSR address allocation, because machine-level CSRs are consolidated and indirect CSR mappings are now listed separately.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Supervisor indirect CSRs were added to the supervisor CSR listing: siselect at 0x150 and sireg, sireg2, sireg3, sireg4, sireg5, and sireg6 at 0x151-0x157.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Supervisor timer compare CSRs were added to the supervisor CSR listing: stimecmp at 0x14D and stimecmph at 0x15D.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: The supervisor resource management configuration CSR srmcfg at 0x181 was added to the supervisor CSR listing.

differences.section: 2.2.2. Currently allocated RISC-V supervisor-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Supervisor Control Transfer Records configuration CSRs were added to the supervisor CSR listing: sctrctl at 0x14E, sctrstatus at 0x14F, and sctrdepth at 0x15F.

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: Virtual supervisor indirect CSRs were added to the hypervisor and VS CSR listing: vsiselect at 0x250 and vsireg, vsireg2, vsireg3, vsireg4, vsireg5, and vsireg6 at 0x251-0x257.

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: Virtual supervisor timer compare CSRs vstimecmp at 0x24D and vstimecmph at 0x25D were added to the hypervisor and VS CSR listing.

differences.section: 2.2.3. Currently allocated RISC-V hypervisor and VS CSR addresses
differences.description: The virtual supervisor CTR control CSR vsctrctl at 0x24E was added to the hypervisor and VS CSR listing.

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Machine indirect CSRs were added to the machine CSR listing: miselect at 0x350 and mireg, mireg2, mireg3, mireg4, mireg5, and mireg6 at 0x351-0x357.

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: Machine counter configuration CSRs mcyclecfg at 0x321, minstretcfg at 0x322, mcyclecfgh at 0x721, and minstretcfgh at 0x722 were added to the machine CSR listing.

differences.section: 2.2.4. Currently allocated RISC-V machine-level CSR addresses
differences.description: The machine CTR control CSR mctrctl at 0x34E was added to the machine CSR listing.

differences.section: 2.2.5. Currently allocated RISC-V indirect CSR (Smcsrind) mappings
differences.description: A new table of M-mode indirect CSR mappings was added, listing miselect values for AIA-related iprio0-iprio15, eidelivery, eithreshold, eip0-eip63, and eie0-eie63.

differences.section: 2.2.5. Currently allocated RISC-V indirect CSR (Smcsrind) mappings
differences.description: A new table of S-mode indirect CSR mappings was added, including AIA mappings, delegated counter mappings for siselect values 0x40-0x5F, and CTR entry mappings for 0x200-0x2FF.

differences.section: 2.2.5. Currently allocated RISC-V indirect CSR (Smcsrind) mappings
differences.description: A new table of VS-mode indirect CSR mappings was added, including AIA mappings and CTR entry mappings for 0x200-0x2FF.

The misa register figure is not rendered in the parsed second document; it appears as the text placeholder "partial$bytefield/misareg.edn" instead of the bit-field diagram shown in the first document.
The sentence "All bits that are reserved for future use must return zero when read" was moved from the caption text before the Extensions table into standalone text before the table.
The commentary changed "the repo around which development occurs" to "the project around which development occurs".
The requirement changed from "at least one hart must have a hart ID of zero" to "one hart must have a hart ID of zero".

differences.section: 3.1.6.2. Double Trap Control in mstatus Register
differences.description: For the critical-error state entered after an unexpected trap, the specification now states that whether performance counters and timers are updated in the critical-error state is UNSPECIFIED.

differences.section: 3.1.6.3. Base ISA Control in mstatus Register
differences.description: The commentary spelling changed from "writeback" to "write-back" for HINT instructions.

The MPRV description was broadened from loads and stores to explicit memory accesses: MPRV now modifies the privilege level at which explicit memory accesses execute, and when MPRV=0 explicit memory accesses behave as normal.
The TW description changed terminology from "lower privilege modes" and "less-privileged modes" to "modes less privileged than M".

The U-mode WFI rule was changed: instead of saying a future revision might allow S-mode to selectively permit WFI in U-mode when TW=0, the second document states that WFI in U-mode causes an illegal-instruction exception regardless of TW unless it completes within a bounded time.

The MSIP description was changed from saying MSIP is written by memory-mapped control registers used by remote harts and that a hart can write its own MSIP bit, to saying MSIP is written by memory-mapped control registers used to provide machine-level interprocessor interrupts.

A definition of a hart's memory-mapped msip register was added: it is a 32-bit read/write register with bits 31-1 reading as zero and bit 0 containing MSIP; changes are reflected in mip.MSIP eventually but not necessarily immediately.

The supervisor timer interrupt bits STIP/STIE description now incorporates Sstc behavior: if stimecmp is implemented, STIP is read-only and reflects the stimecmp timer interrupt signal; otherwise STIP remains writable by M-mode software.

The machine CSR listing now includes mcyclecfg/minstretcfg, and the hardware performance monitor text has been aligned with that listing; the second document also contains a typo "bitj 31-0" where the first document said "bits 31-0".

The mcounteren.TM bit description now also controls access to stimecmp and vstimecmp: if TM is clear, attempts to access these registers below M-mode raise illegal-instruction exceptions; if set, access is permitted subject to Sstc and hcounteren rules.
The list of exceptions for which a nonzero mtval contains the faulting virtual address was expanded to include hardware-error exceptions.

A new rule states that on a breakpoint exception raised by EBREAK or C.EBREAK, mtval is written with either zero or the virtual address of the instruction, with commentary explaining the redundancy with mepc and backwards compatibility.
The misaligned load/store and variable-length instruction cases for mtval were expanded to include hardware-error exceptions in addition to access-fault and page-fault exceptions.
The pointer-alignment formula was completed: the second document states that if MXLEN is 8×n, then mconfigptr[log2 n-1:0] must be zero; the first document had missing mathematical operands.

A synchronization rule was added for ADUE: after changing menvcfg.ADUE, executing SFENCE.VMA with rs1=x0 and rs2=x0 suffices to synchronize address-translation caches with respect to the altered interpretation of PTE A/D bits, with hypervisor synchronization referenced separately.

The STCE field definition changed from a pointer to the Sstc extension to an inline definition: STCE enables stimecmp for S-mode, is read-only zero when Sstc is absent, and controls illegal-instruction behavior and STIP fallback when clear.
The CBZE field definition changed from a pointer to the Zicboz extension to an inline definition controlling whether CBO.ZERO may execute below M-mode.

The CBCFE and CBIE field definitions changed from a pointer to the Zicbom extension to inline definitions controlling CBO.CLEAN, CBO.FLUSH, and CBO.INVAL below M-mode, including CBIE encodings 00, 01, 10 reserved, and 11.

The PMM field definition changed from a pointer to the Smnpm extension to an inline definition with a legal-value table for pointer masking and a statement that PMM is read-only zero for RV32 or when Smnpm is absent.
mseccfg changed from "an optional 64-bit read/write register" to a 64-bit read/write register that exists if any extension adding an mseccfg field is implemented; otherwise it is reserved.
Detailed definitions for the Zkr SSEED and USEED fields were added, including seed CSR access rules for M, U, S/HS, and VS/VU modes and an entropy source access-control table.

Detailed definitions for Smepmp fields RLB, MMWP, and MML were added to the mseccfg section, including sticky behavior, locked PMP rule modification, machine-mode allowlist policy, Machine Mode Lockdown, M-mode-only and S/U-mode-only rules, and Shared-Region encodings.

The PMM field definition for Smmpm was added to mseccfg, including a legal-value table for pointer masking, RV32 read-only-zero behavior, and a note about satisfying address-size constraints to avoid TLB access-path masking logic.
The mseccfgh existence rule was clarified: mseccfgh exists when XLEN=32 and mseccfg is implemented, and does not exist when XLEN=64.
Reset behavior for mseccfg.MML, mseccfg.MMWP, and mseccfg.RLB was added: they are set to 0 unless the platform mandates a different reset value.
Reset behavior for mseccfg.USEED and mseccfg.SSEED was added: they must have defined reset values and must not be left undefined after reset.
AMOArithmetic was clarified to indicate support for all RISC-V AMOs defined by the A extension, rather than all RISC-V AMOs generally.
Zacas PMA support levels AMOCASW, AMOCASD, and AMOCASQ were added, describing support for AMOCAS.W, AMOCAS.D, and AMOCAS.Q on top of AMOArithmetic.
A note was added that AMOCASW/D/Q require AMOArithmetic support because AMOCAS.W/D/Q need arithmetic comparison and swap capability.
A rule was added that AMOs specified by Zabha require the same PMA support level as corresponding A-extension or Zacas instructions.
The set of instructions covered by the misaligned atomicity granule PMA was expanded to include compressed encodings of the listed AMOs, loads, and stores.
PMP permission-fault rules now explicitly include cache-block management instructions as read accesses and cache-block zero instructions as write accesses.

A note was added explaining that TOR PMP cannot represent address 2^(XLEN+2) as the top of a range, so the uppermost word of memory cannot be granted by TOR in systems such as RV32 with 34-bit physical addresses.
The sentence stating that if the current XLEN is greater than MXLEN, PMP address registers are zero-extended from MXLEN to XLEN for address matching was removed.
The L-bit permission statement was changed to say permissions are "additionally enforced" on M-mode accesses when L is set.

PMP priority and matching logic was rewritten in terms of individual memory operations rather than accesses, clarifying behavior for decomposed misaligned accesses and instruction-generated multiple memory operations.

The discussion of misaligned loads, stores, and instruction fetches being decomposed into multiple operations was moved to the start of the priority section and now explicitly references the RVWMO specification.

The RV32 description was changed from saying the listed state-enable registers are 32-bit to saying there are CSR addresses for accessing the upper 32 bits of corresponding machine-level and hypervisor CSRs.

The SRMCFG bit description was shortened from saying it controls access to the srmcfg CSR introduced by the Ssqosid Chapter 18 extension to saying it controls access to the srmcfg CSR introduced by the Ssqosid extension.
The introductory text was rewritten to remove the explicit SMAP and SMEP terminology and describe the two protective mechanisms more generally.

The Smepmp chapter was reorganized: the first document had sections 6.1 Introduction, 6.1.1 Threat model, 6.2 Proposal, 6.2.1 Truth table, 6.2.2 Visual representation, 6.3 Software discovery, and 6.4 Rationale; the second document has 6.1 Threat model, 6.2 Smepmp Physical Memory Protection Rules, and 6.3 Software discovery, with rationale moved to Appendix A.
A sentence was added stating that the rationale guiding development of Smepmp is included in Appendix A.1.
The threat model now explicitly says "Without the Smepmp extension" before describing the inability to enforce PMP rules only on non-Machine modes and deny them in Machine mode.
The mitigation wording changed from attacks mitigated through "SMAP/SMEP" to attacks mitigated through "the virtual-memory system".
The numbered proposal text defining mseccfg, RLB, MMWP, and MML was removed from the Smepmp chapter; the detailed specification is now in the mseccfg section.
The Smepmp rationale section was removed from Chapter 6 and no longer appears there.

differences.section: Appendix A.1. "Smepmp" Extension for PMP Enhancements for memory access and execution prevention in Machine mode
differences.description: A new appendix was added containing historical rationale for the Smepmp extension, largely replacing the former Chapter 6 rationale text.
The paragraph listing CSR numbers 0x321, 0x322, 0x721, and 0x722 for mcyclecfg, minstretcfg, mcyclecfgh, and minstretcfgh was removed from this section; these numbers are now in the CSR listing.
The description of masking mnepc[1] when IALIGN=32 was corrected to say the masking occurs for the implicit read by MNRET; the first document incorrectly said MRET.
The chapter title changed from "Smcdeleg" Counter Delegation Extension to "Smcdeleg/Ssccfg" Counter Delegation Extensions.
The introductory text now consistently refers to extensions in the plural and adds that Smcdeleg and Ssccfg must always be implemented in tandem for a RISC-V hardware platform.

The note that hpmeventi/cyclecfg/instretcfg represent subsets of machine state was expanded into normative text specifying that if Sscofpmf or Smcntrpmf is implemented, bit 62 (MINH) is read-only zero when accessed through sireg*.
The note identifying Smaia/Ssaia registers was expanded to include mvip in addition to mvien and hvien.
A sentence was added specifying that for a not-taken branch, ctrtarget holds the PC of the next sequential instruction following the branch.
The commentary changed from "Transfer Type Filtering bits" to "Transfer Type Filter bits" in describing the ctrdata.TYPE encoding source.
The STIP description now incorporates Sstc behavior: when Sstc is implemented, STIP reflects the timer interrupt signal from stimecmp and is set or cleared by writing stimecmp relative to time.

A new supervisor CSR section for stimecmp was added, specifying its 64-bit precision, RV32 stimecmph high-half access, pending supervisor timer interrupt behavior, eventual STIP reflection, and spurious interrupt note.
The list of exceptions for which nonzero stval contains the faulting virtual address was expanded to include hardware-error exceptions.
A new rule states that on a breakpoint exception raised by EBREAK or C.EBREAK, stval is written with either zero or the virtual address of the instruction.
The misaligned load/store and instruction access cases for stval were expanded to include hardware-error exceptions.
The CBZE field definition changed from a pointer to Zicboz to an inline definition controlling U-mode CBO.ZERO execution and requiring S-mode enablement.

The CBCFE and CBIE field definitions changed from pointers to Zicbom to inline definitions controlling U-mode CBO.CLEAN, CBO.FLUSH, and CBO.INVAL execution, including CBIE encodings and flush-vs-invalidate behavior.
The PMM field definition changed from a pointer to Ssnpm to an inline pointer-masking definition with a legal-value table and RV32 read-only-zero behavior.

The commentary about using translations since the most recent SFENCE.VMA was expanded: the second document states that any older translation since the last subsuming SFENCE.VMA may be used, subsequent accesses may use different translations, and translations from different virtual-address widths or global translations may be used.

A rule was added stating that no SFENCE.VMA is required after enabling or disabling pointer masking because pointer masking affects only the effective address and not memory-management data structures.
Page permission checks now explicitly include cache-block management instructions as load-like accesses and cache-block zero instructions as store-like accesses.
The A/D-bit update requirement changed wording from "tablewalk checks" to "page-table walk checks".

Step 9 now specifies that the PMA/PMP check is for a store to the PTE at address a+va.vpn[i]×PTESIZE, and explicitly says to store the updated pte back to that PTE address after setting A and optionally D.
The Sv48 supported superpage sizes are now explicitly named as 4 KiB pages, 2 MiB megapages, 1 GiB gigapages, and 512 GiB terapages; the first document used only generic names.
The Sv57 supported superpage sizes are now explicitly named as 4 KiB pages, 2 MiB megapages, 1 GiB gigapages, 512 GiB terapages, and 256 TiB petapages; the first document used only generic names.

differences.section: 12.7. "Svnapot" Extension for NAPOT Translation Contiguity, Version 1.0
differences.description: A sentence was added stating that if the hypervisor extension is also implemented, Svnapot is also supported in G-stage translation.

differences.section: 12.12. "Svrsw60t59b" Extension for PTE Reserved-for-Software Bits 60-59, Version 1.0
differences.description: A new Svrsw60t59b extension section was added, reserving PTE bits 60-59 for supervisor software and requiring implementations to ignore them.

differences.section: 12.12. "Svrsw60t59b" Extension for PTE Reserved-for-Software Bits 60-59, Version 1.0
differences.description: The Svrsw60t59b section adds that if the Hypervisor extension is implemented, bits 60-59 of G-stage PTEs are reserved for supervisor software and ignored by the implementation.

differences.section: 12.12. "Svrsw60t59b" Extension for PTE Reserved-for-Software Bits 60-59, Version 1.0
differences.description: The Svrsw60t59b section adds that the extension depends on Sv39 and includes commentary explaining operating-system use of reserved PTE bits for metadata and the prior fault behavior for nonzero bits 60-59.

differences.section: 13. "Sstc" Extension for Supervisor-mode Timer Interrupts, Version 1.0
differences.description: The Sstc chapter was reduced from detailed subsections for stimecmp, mip/mie, sip/sie, mcounteren, vstimecmp, hip/hie, hcounteren, and environment config support to a short summary pointing to the new integrated sections for stimecmp, vstimecmp, menvcfg.STCE, and henvcfg.STCE.

differences.section: 13. "Sstc" Extension for Supervisor-mode Timer Interrupts, Version 1.0
differences.description: The explicit CSR-number statement for stimecmp/stimecmph at 0x14D/0x15D was removed from the Sstc chapter; these numbers now appear in the CSR listing.

differences.section: 13. "Sstc" Extension for Supervisor-mode Timer Interrupts, Version 1.0
differences.description: The detailed modification text for mip.STIP/mie.STIE and sip.STIP/sie.STIE was removed from the Sstc chapter and incorporated into the machine and supervisor interrupt sections.

differences.section: 13. "Sstc" Extension for Supervisor-mode Timer Interrupts, Version 1.0
differences.description: The detailed modification text for mcounteren.TM and hcounteren.TM was removed from the Sstc chapter and incorporated into the mcounteren and hcounteren sections.

differences.section: 13. "Sstc" Extension for Supervisor-mode Timer Interrupts, Version 1.0
differences.description: The explicit CSR-number statement for vstimecmp/vstimecmph at 0x24D/0x25D was removed from the Sstc chapter; these numbers now appear in the CSR listing.
A definition of the hstatus.HUPMM field was added for Ssnpm, controlling pointer masking for HLV.* and HSV.* instructions in U-mode when their explicit memory access is performed as VU-mode.
A note was added that the hypervisor should copy the value written to senvcfg.PMM by the guest to hstatus.HUPMM before invoking HLV.* or HSV.* instructions in U-mode.
The HUPMM field is specified to be read-only zero when Ssnpm is not implemented and read-only zero for RV32.

differences.section: 15.2.3. Hypervisor Interrupt (hvip, hip, and hie) Registers
differences.description: The VSTIP description now incorporates Sstc behavior: hip.VSTIP is the logical OR of hvip.VSTIP and, when Sstc is implemented, the timer interrupt signal from vstimecmp; it is set or cleared by writes to vstimecmp relative to time+htimedelta.

The STCE field definition changed from a pointer to the Sstc extension to an inline definition enabling vstimecmp for VS-mode and describing virtual-instruction behavior and VSTIP fallback when clear.

The CBZE, CBCFE, and CBIE field definitions changed from pointers to Zicboz/Zicbom to inline definitions controlling CBO.ZERO, CBO.CLEAN, CBO.FLUSH, and CBO.INVAL in VS/VU modes, including HS-qualified checks and virtual-instruction exceptions.
The PMM field definition changed from a pointer to Ssnpm to an inline definition controlling pointer masking for VS-mode with a legal-value table and RV32 read-only-zero behavior.

A rule was added for hcounteren.TM: if clear, VS-mode access to vstimecmp via stimecmp causes a virtual-instruction exception when mcounteren.TM is set; if both bits are set, VS-mode access to vstimecmp is permitted if implemented.

differences.section: 15.2.18. Virtual Supervisor Address Translation and Protection (vsatp) Register
differences.description: The note about virtual-machine load/store instructions was changed from "misspeculatively executed" to "mispredicted" when describing that they must not cause VS-stage A bits to be set.

A new hypervisor CSR section for vstimecmp was added, specifying its 64-bit precision, RV32 vstimecmph high-half access, VSTIP pending behavior based on time+htimedelta, eventual reflection, and SBI compatibility note.

differences.section: 15.3.1. Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions
differences.description: A rule was added that memory accesses performed by HLVX.* instructions are not subject to pointer masking.

differences.section: 15.3.1. Hypervisor Virtual-Machine Load and Store Instructions
differences.description: A note was added explaining that HLVX.* instructions emulate implicit instruction-fetch accesses from guest memory and are exempt from pointer masking; the note also states that pointer masking does not apply when MXR is set.

The G bit in G-stage PTEs changed from "reserved for future standard use" to "currently not used", while retaining the requirement that software clear it for forward compatibility and hardware ignore it.

Synchronization requirements for environment configuration changes were expanded from PBMTE only to PBMTE or ADUE in both menvcfg and henvcfg, covering altered interpretation of PBMT and A/D bit fields.

A new hypervisor section was added describing interaction of pointer masking with two-stage address translation, including GPAs being two bits wider than corresponding virtual address modes and the special case of VS/VU execution with vsatp.MODE=Bare.

The new pointer-masking interaction section adds hypervisor guidance to execute HFENCE.GVMA with rs1=x0 when henvcfg.PMM changes to or from values where XLEN-PMLEN is less than the GPA width for Sv57x4 or Sv48x4 cases.
In the hypervisor trap-cause table, interrupt cause 13 changed from "Reserved for counter-overflow interrupt" to "Counter-overflow interrupt".

differences.section: 16.1.2. Preserving Expected Landing Pad State on Traps
differences.description: The phrase "an software-check exception" was corrected to "a software-check exception", and the obsolete parenthetical reference "See [LP_INST]" was removed.

The Bare-mode rule was narrowed and rephrased: instead of saying shadow stack memory accesses are prohibited below M-mode, the second document says shadow stack instructions raise a store/AMO access-fault exception when satp.MODE or vsatp.MODE is Bare and the effective privilege mode is less than M.
The commentary was edited to say "access fault" instead of "access-fault" when describing why shadow-stack violations use access faults rather than page faults.
The list of privilege modes where pointer-masking safety tools can be applied was punctuated as "U, S, and M" instead of "U, S and M".
The definition of effective address changed "page table walks" to "page-table walks".

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: The compressed-instruction list subject to pointer masking no longer includes C.LQSP or C.SQSP in the second document.

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: The hardware-write and debug-trigger rules now state that pointer masking is applied "when applicable"; the first document stated unconditionally that pointer masking is applied for hardware writes to CSRs and debug address-trigger matching.

differences.section: 18.2.6. Memory Accesses Subject to Pointer Masking
differences.description: The example of stvec/stval behavior now says hardware applies pointer masking to an address written into stval only if pointer masking is applicable to that address.
The extension descriptions for Ssnpm, Smnpm, and Smmpm now include cross-references to the CSR sections that define their controls, instead of saying details are in the ISA Extensions section.
The sentence "See Section 24.3 for details on how each of these extensions is configured" was removed.
The RV32/UXL/SXL/MXL paragraph now references the specific CSR sections for details rather than Section 24.3.

The standalone Pointer Masking ISA Extensions section was removed, including the general PMM WARL-field table and the Ssnpm, Smnpm, Smmpm, interaction with SFENCE.VMA, interaction with two-stage address translation, and number-of-masked-bits subsections.

The standalone Ssnpm subsection defining senvcfg.PMM, henvcfg.PMM, hstatus.HUPMM, and HLV/HSV/HLVX pointer masking behavior was removed; this material is now integrated into the relevant CSR and hypervisor instruction sections.
The standalone Smnpm subsection defining menvcfg.PMM was removed; this material is now integrated into the menvcfg section.
The standalone Smmpm subsection defining mseccfg.PMM was removed; this material is now integrated into the mseccfg section.
The standalone statement that no SFENCE.VMA is required after enabling/disabling pointer masking was removed from the Pointer Masking chapter; the rule is now in the SFENCE.VMA section.
The standalone two-stage address translation interaction subsection was removed from the Pointer Masking chapter; the material is now in the Hypervisor chapter as Interaction with Pointer Masking.
The Number of Masked Bits content was moved from section 24.3.6 in the first document to section 18.2.8 in the second document.
The privileged instruction listing now includes a new "Control Transfer Records Management Instructions" group with the SCTRCLR instruction encoding 0001000 00100 00000 000 00000 1110011.

The standalone Bibliography chapter from the first document was removed as a separate chapter; in the second document the bibliography entries appear at the end of Appendix A.1 after the text "== Bibliography".
